sn10.12:1.2: that on one occasion the Blessed One was staying near Āḷavī in the haunt of the Āḷavaka yakkha.
sn10.12:1.3: Then the Āḷavaka yakkha went to the Blessed One and on arrival said to him:
sn10.12:1.5: (Saying,) “All right, my friend,” the Blessed One went out.
sn10.12:1.7: (Saying,) “All right, my friend,” the Blessed One went in.
sn10.12:1.13: A third time, the Āḷavaka yakkha said to the Blessed One,
sn10.12:1.15: (Saying,) “All right, my friend,” the Blessed One went out.
sn10.12:1.17: (Saying,) “All right, my friend,” the Blessed One went in.
sn10.12:1.18: Then a fourth time, the Āḷavaka yakkha said to the Blessed One,
sn10.12:1.21: Do what you have to do.”
sn10.12:1.22: “I will ask you a question, contemplative.
sn10.12:1.23: If you can’t answer me, I will possess your mind or rip open your heart or, grabbing you by the feet, hurl you across the Ganges.”
sn10.12:1.24: “My friend, I see no one in the cosmos with its devas, Māras & Brahmās, its contemplatives & brahmans, its royalty & commonfolk, who could possess my mind or rip open my heart or, grabbing me by the feet, hurl me across the Ganges.
sn10.12:1.25: But nevertheless, ask me what you wish.”
sn10.12:2.1: Āḷavaka: “What is a person’s highest wealth?
sn10.12:2.3: What is the highest of savors?
sn10.12:2.4: Living in what way is one’s life called the best?”
sn10.12:3.1: The Buddha: “Conviction is a person’s highest wealth.
sn10.12:3.3: Truth is the highest of savors.1
sn10.12:3.4: Living with discernment, one’s life is called best.”
sn10.12:4.1: Āḷavaka: “How does one cross over the flood?
sn10.12:4.3: How does one overcome suffering & stress?
sn10.12:5.1: The Buddha: “Through conviction one crosses over the flood.
sn10.12:5.3: Through persistence one overcomes suffering & stress.
sn10.12:5.4: Through discernment a person is purified.”
sn10.12:6.5: Passing from this world to the next world,
sn10.12:7.1: The Buddha: “Convinced of the arahants’ Dhamma
sn10.12:10.3: if anything better than truth, self-control,
sn10.12:10.4: stamina, & relinquishment here can be found.”
sn10.12:12.1: It was truly for my well-being
sn10.12:12.2: that the Awakened One came to stay in Āḷavī.
sn10.12:12.4: where what is given bears great fruit.
sn10.12:13.2: from village to village, town to town,
sn10.12:13.3: paying homage to the Self-awakened One
sn10.12:13.4: & the true rightness of the Dhamma.”
